newsSinceJun6: No company-specific fundamental news identified since June 6. The June 9 +9.95% limit-up to a record NT$4,475 appears flow/momentum-driven - continuation of the Google TPU v8i (Zebrafish) ASIC rerating (cloud ASIC 2026 guidance doubled to ~$2B on May 29; street modeling $7-12B for 2027) amid broad Taiex record highs, not a new datapoint. CEO Rick Tsai has separately conceded MediaTek is a 'latecomer' in CPO with demand only essential around 2028-29.

sources.label: BigGo Finance (May 29) - MediaTek hikes 2026 AI ASIC revenue forecast to $2B
UDN Money - Rick Tsai on TPU demand and CPO 'latecomer' positioning
DIGITIMES - MediaTek ASIC revenue may overtake smartphone chips on Google TPU ramp
TechSpot - MediaTek's billion-dollar pivot from smartphone chips to AI ASICs

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already flagged removal for weak direct HBM/advanced-packaging/CPO bottleneck relevance; today's ~10% limit-up on no identifiable new fundamentals since June 6 (+204% YTD, $226B cap) further compresses residual alpha on this bottleneck-criticality list.
verdicts.bull: Google TPU v8i design win and a doubled $2B 2026 cloud ASIC guide make MediaTek a genuine second ASIC source to Broadcom, with $7-12B 2027 revenue potential transforming the earnings mix.
verdicts.neutral: The ASIC story is real but fully broadcast and priced; at rank 73 with an 'analog/power/RF' tier tag the listing is a category mismatch, and the name no longer offers hidden bottleneck exposure this page rewards.
verdicts.bear: Stock has tripled since early April on a program whose mass production only starts H2 2026; any Google TPU spec revision, share shift back to Broadcom, or smartphone-cycle weakness hits a now-crowded $226B name hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: TPU v8i mass production ramp and first separate ASIC revenue disclosure confirming the $2B 2026 / multi-billion 2027 trajectory.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded post-rerating positioning: a 204% YTD, limit-up-on-no-news chart leaves severe drawdown risk if TPU order timing or volumes disappoint.

verdicts.rankRationale: On a CPO-lens alpha page, CEO Rick Tsai's own admission that MediaTek is a CPO 'latecomer' (demand essential only ~2028-29, Ayar Labs stake as a placeholder) confirms it is not a primary CPO bottleneck, while today's no-news limit-up removes what little asymmetry remained.
verdicts.bull: ASIC scale from Google TPU gives MediaTek the silicon platform and customer relationships to attach CPO/optical I/O when the 2028-29 wave arrives, with the Ayar Labs investment and Microsoft optical research as on-ramps.
verdicts.neutral: CPO exposure is optionality two-plus cycles out; near-term value is the TPU ASIC ramp, which is already the consensus narrative and orthogonal to this page's thesis.
verdicts.bear: A self-described CPO latecomer trading at record highs after a 3x two-month run offers negative alpha on this list; incumbents (Broadcom, TSMC COUPE ecosystem, optics specialists) own the actual CPO bottleneck.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Concrete CPO/optical-I/O design win or co-package milestone with a hyperscaler ASIC program moving the 2028-29 optionality forward.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Page-thesis mismatch plus crowding: paying a post-rerating ASIC multiple for CPO exposure the company itself dates to 2028-29.
